subject: Geography
title: The Sierra Madre
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Sierra Madre usable.

hook.text: Mexico's Sierra Madre ranges frame the country like two walls running north to south.

buildup.text: The Sierra Madre Occidental holds Copper Canyon—deeper and larger than the Grand Canyon.

discovery.text: The Tarahumara people live in these canyons, famous for running ultra-distances barefoot.

twist.text: Drug cartels now use the Sierra Madre's isolation to hide operations in remote valleys.

climax.text: The mountains are rich in silver—Mexico became the world's top silver producer because of them.

punchline.text: Mountains of silver, canyons deeper than the Grand.

quiz.question: What is notable about Mexico's Copper Canyon?
options: It is deeper and larger than the Grand Canyon
It contains the most copper on Earth
It was carved by a volcano
